facts.shortSummary: At the Leviathan Inn, Raina tells the party that the ocean's song has returned and begs them to recover a trapped fragment of it from a hidden shrine near Hamri.
facts.longSummary: The session opens with a broad recap of Hamri's recent history before Wazir gathers Ryu, Trok, Nerissa, and Ander at the Leviathan Inn to hear out a storm-battered sailor named Raina. She explains that she has heard the ocean speak in visions since childhood, that the songs fell silent years ago after a nightmare of tearing and loss, and that only recently they returned with a plea about something trapped under rock yet still resting on the waves. The voice showed her a shrine of Guzu the Mariner and begged for a fragment of song to be restored, so she has come to Hamri asking for help from people she has been told can be trusted.

location.context: common room gathering as Wazir hears out a newly arrived sailor
location.notes: The whole opening scene stays inside Wazir's inn while Raina explains why she came to Hamri.

npcs.name: Raina
npcs.notes: She asks the party to recover the lost fragment and return it to its home.

npcs.name: Caleb
npcs.notes: He raised Raina on stories of Mawar and was uneasy when the ocean's voice first went silent.

npcs.name: Kaiso
npcs.context: Chardonian scholar recently staying in Hamri
npcs.notes: He appears in the session recap of Hamri's recent troubles and rumors from Chardon.

npcs.name: Guzu the Mariner
npcs.context: Mawarin saint seen in Raina's visions
npcs.notes: Raina's picture of the hidden place centers on a shrine to Guzu.

items.name: fragment of song
items.notes: Raina describes a lost fragment of the ocean's voice trapped somewhere in the cliffs near Hamri.

organizations.name: Ocean Watchers
organizations.notes: Raina grew up on stories about them and Hamri's strange relationship with the sea.

facts.shortSummary: The party questions Raina, consults Yaz and Ovina, and learns that the vision likely points to a long-buried shrine of Guzu in the sea caves of Hamri's North Cliff.
facts.longSummary: Still at the Leviathan, the group presses Raina for more detail and works from Trok's sketch of the place shown in her vision. Yaz recognizes the picture as matching an old shrine of Guzu the Mariner once built in a sea cave on the North Cliff, while Ovina, an old dwarf who has been in Hamri for more than a century, remembers the cave and confirms it collapsed long ago near the last tower in the line. With their clues aligned around a buried coastal shrine and a likely low-tide approach, the party settles on taking Ryu's boat out in the morning to search the site directly.

location.context: extended question-and-answer session with local experts summoned to the inn
location.notes: The inquiry stays in the tavern while the party assembles enough local memory to identify the North Cliff shrine.

npcs.name: Raina
npcs.notes: She confirms the blue-lit cave shrine and the need to recover the fragment.

npcs.name: Yaz
npcs.context: elderly local source on Hamri's older shrines
npcs.notes: He identifies the vision as matching a Guzu shrine once built in a North Cliff sea cave.

npcs.name: Ovina
npcs.context: long-lived dwarf blacksmith with old harbor memory
npcs.notes: She remembers the cave, places it near the last tower, and says it collapsed long ago.

npcs.name: Guzu the Mariner
npcs.context: saint honored by the lost shrine
npcs.notes: Both the sketch and the local testimony point to a cave shrine dedicated to Guzu.

items.name: Trok's shrine sketch
items.notes: The party uses Trok's drawing from Raina's description to question Yaz and Ovina.

items.name: fragment of song
items.notes: The group's entire search is framed around recovering the trapped fragment Raina described.

facts.shortSummary: The next morning the party sails to the North Cliff, finds the old collapse, and confirms that a blasted opening now leads back into the buried shrine cave.
facts.longSummary: After sleeping on the plan, the group meets the next morning, takes breakfast, and boards Ryu's small boat with Raina along for the ride. They follow the North Cliff shoreline to the place Ovina described, use scouting and shared checks to study the rockfall, and realize that the cave has recently been punched open again by some force far cleaner than a natural collapse. With Ryu linked through Turk's senses and Wazir's mechanical fish scouting alongside, they confirm there is still a navigable way into the old shrine beyond the rubble.

location.to: North Cliff sea cave outside Hamri
location.context: morning boat trip and shoreline search for the collapsed Guzu shrine
location.notes: The beat ends once the party identifies the blasted passage back into the cave.

npcs.name: Raina
npcs.context: sailor accompanying the search by boat
npcs.notes: She joins the ride out and helps confirm that the party has reached the place from her vision.

facts.shortSummary: Swimming through the breach, the party finds the blue-lit beach and ruined outer temple of Guzu's shrine, spots idle water creatures, and advances toward the building.
facts.longSummary: The party dives through the newly blasted underwater opening and surfaces inside a hidden sea cave whose interior matches Raina's vision: a warm blue glow, a strip of sandy beach, and a white shrine building on the shore. They quickly realize the collapse was not the only recent disturbance, because the breach and the shattered doorway deeper inside both look as if magic blasted straight through stone and timber. A cluster of watery, frog-like shrine creatures loiters around the beach and temple, and after sizing up the scene the party presses inward instead of retreating for a longer plan.

location.primary: buried sea cave shrine of Guzu (North Cliff)
location.context: sandy inner beach and ruined outer temple beyond the blasted underwater passage
location.notes: This is the first direct entry into the hidden shrine cave Raina saw in her vision.

items.name: shattered reinforced door
items.notes: Broken chunks of the inner doorway show that something recently blasted deeper into the shrine.

facts.shortSummary: Inside the shrine, the party uncovers books and offerings, finds the ancestor prayer that opens a hidden stone passage, and reveals a trapped water elemental clutching the crystal fragment.
facts.longSummary: With the outer approach secured for the moment, the party searches the main temple and the side chapel in earnest. They study the statue of Guzu, the glowing altar, scattered offerings, and a damaged stone chest that still preserves a prayer book, a saintly story collection, and a ledger of prayers left at the shrine. The Mawarin inscription about kneeling and praying Guzu's ancestor prayer leads Nerissa to the right text, and when she sings it the hidden slab door vanishes to expose a deeper chamber. Inside waits a large water elemental figure pinned by the shrine's blue light and holding the crystal fragment the party came to recover.

location.context: main sanctuary, side chapel, and newly revealed hidden chamber
location.notes: The beat tracks the party from temple investigation to the revelation of the trapped inner-chamber intruder.

npcs.name: Guzu the Mariner
npcs.context: saint honored by the shrine's altar, prayer, and inscriptions
npcs.notes: The hidden passage opens only through Guzu's ancestor prayer.

npcs.name: Gazankoa
npcs.context: power invoked by the trapped elemental's minion
npcs.notes: The elemental's servant blurts out that the crystal is meant for Gazankoa.

items.name: prayer book
items.notes: A preserved book in the chapel chest contains Guzu's ancestor prayer.

items.name: saints' story book
items.notes: An illuminated collection of local saint stories survives in the same stone chest.

items.name: prayer ledger
items.notes: A damaged guestbook preserves prayers and hopes once left at the shrine.

items.name: song fragment crystal
items.notes: The hidden chamber contains the crystal fragment the ocean wants restored.

facts.shortSummary: The trapped elemental offers to spare the party if they free it and let it take the crystal to Gazankoa, but the blue light's binding effect and the creature's threats convince the group to fight instead.
facts.longSummary: Before steel is drawn, the party talks with the elemental in the hidden chamber and learns how badly things have gone inside the shrine. The creature explains that it forced its way in, seized the crystal fragment, and triggered the blue light bound to the altar, which now prevents its teleportation and keeps it trapped despite the opened passage. It offers to return to the plane of water and even let the party present the crystal to Gazankoa, but the more it talks the clearer it becomes that it will simply keep hunting the shard and anyone carrying it. By the end of the exchange, the party settles on bottling the creature up inside the shrine and killing it rather than trusting any bargain.

location.context: threshold between the glowing altar room and the elemental's prison chamber
location.notes: The whole beat is a tense negotiation at the doorway before initiative begins.

npcs.name: Gazankoa
npcs.context: the power the elemental claims to serve
npcs.notes: The elemental frames the crystal as something that should be brought to Gazankoa.

items.name: song fragment crystal
items.notes: The crystal remains in the elemental's grasp during the negotiation and drives the whole argument.

facts.shortSummary: The elemental opens the fight with a brutal jet of water, hurling the party back, dropping Geo, and turning the doorway ambush into a hard scramble for control.
facts.longSummary: When the parley breaks down, initiative starts and the trapped elemental wastes no time, lashing the shrine interior with a massive jet of frigid water. The opening blast batters Trok, Ryu, Ander, Geo, and the nearby shrine creatures, immediately showing how dangerous the foe is even while confined by the blue light. Ryu recovers enough to answer with thrown trident strikes and Hunter's Mark, Ander fights his way back into the doorway, and the party's neat plan to contain the creature turns into a messy opening brawl in the temple.

location.context: altar room and doorway to the hidden chamber during the opening combat exchange
location.notes: The fight begins with the party clustered near the threshold and stairs while the elemental attacks from deeper inside.

mainEnemies.name: trapped water elemental
mainEnemies.notes: The crystal-holding intruder trapped by the shrine's blue light opens the fight with overwhelming force.

mainEnemies.name: shrine water creatures
mainEnemies.notes: The lesser watery creatures around the shrine support the elemental during the first rounds.

combat.notes: The opening rounds are defined by the elemental's water jet, the party's disrupted formation, and the sudden realization that the fight will not be quick.

facts.shortSummary: The shrine's blue light begins to work against the elemental, and Nerissa's magic drives it back into the chamber while the party piles on damage.
facts.longSummary: As the melee settles into its middle phase, the same blue glow binding the elemental starts actively helping the party. Nerissa's dissonant whispers bite especially hard under the altar's influence and force the elemental backward into the chamber, where opportunity attacks from Trok and Wazir leave it staggering and bloodied. The lesser shrine creatures rush the stairs and doorway, but the party keeps pressure on the main foe and turns the cramped temple layout into an advantage instead of a trap.

location.context: altar platform, doorway, and stair choke point as the party drives the elemental backward
location.notes: The blue-lit sanctuary itself becomes part of the party's advantage during this phase.

mainEnemies.name: trapped water elemental
mainEnemies.notes: The main foe is bloodied and visibly hindered by the shrine's blue light.

mainEnemies.name: shrine water creatures
mainEnemies.notes: The lesser creatures surge forward to hold the stairs and shield their commander.

combat.notes: The middle turns when the shrine's magic and Nerissa's spell combine to drive the elemental back into a worse position.

facts.shortSummary: Desperate and badly hurt, the elemental fights through grapples and minion pressure, turns to mist to flee the chamber, and nearly slips away while the party holds the beach-temple choke point.
facts.longSummary: The battle stretches on with more boiling water, lightning-laced whip attacks, and close-quarters grappling around the altar and stairs. The lesser creatures keep harrying Ryu and Ander while Nerissa and the others carve away at the elemental's remaining strength, and eventually the main foe abandons a straight fight for a desperate escape. It dissolves into watery mist and moves through the wall, trying to reposition and survive, but the party keeps enough control of the shrine entrance and beach approach to prevent a clean withdrawal.

location.context: altar room, beach approach, and wall-side escape route during the collapsing late phase of the fight
location.notes: By the end of the beat the elemental has shifted into a fleeing posture rather than trying to win outright.

mainEnemies.name: trapped water elemental
mainEnemies.notes: Badly wounded, the elemental resorts to mist form and retreat rather than holding the chamber.

mainEnemies.name: remaining shrine water creatures
mainEnemies.notes: The surviving lesser creatures keep grappling and lashing out while the elemental tries to escape.

combat.notes: The core tension in this phase is whether the elemental can slip away through the shrine before the party finishes it.

facts.shortSummary: The party kills the fleeing elemental and the last shrine creatures, recovers the crystal, returns it to Raina, and learns the fragment belongs in the merfolk city of Omni.
facts.longSummary: The final exchanges of combat end with the elemental deflating into a dying swirl of water and the last shrine creatures cut down before they can swim off after Raina. In the quiet that follows, Nerissa hears the crystal sing with gratitude and longing, and the party confirms there is little else of value left in the ruined shrine beyond the offerings Trok already gathered. They swim back out to Raina, hand over the recovered fragment, and all of them share a vision of an undersea home being rebuilt among kelp and fishfolk. Raina recognizes the destination as the merfolk city of Omni somewhere off the coast of Vaporia, takes responsibility for carrying the crystal onward, and returns with the party to the Leviathan Inn to prepare for departure the next morning.

location.context: aftermath at the shrine gives way to the crystal handoff at Raina's boat and a return to town
location.notes: The beat begins in the shrine's aftermath and ends with Raina settling in at the Leviathan before sailing for Omni.

npcs.name: Raina
npcs.context: sailor entrusted with the recovered crystal
npcs.notes: She accepts the fragment and recognizes the vision of its destination.

npcs.name: Gazankoa
npcs.context: power invoked in the elemental's dying threat and the session epilogue
npcs.notes: The elemental dies promising Gazankoa will hear of the party's interference.

items.name: song fragment crystal
items.notes: The recovered crystal sings gratitude and longing for its undersea home once the fight is over.

mainEnemies.name: trapped water elemental
mainEnemies.notes: The fleeing elemental is finally finished off as it tries to regroup outside the chamber.

mainEnemies.name: remaining shrine water creatures
mainEnemies.notes: The last lesser creatures are killed before they can swim off and threaten Raina.

combat.notes: The fight ends quickly once the elemental drops, and the rest of the beat moves straight into recovery of the crystal and the handoff to Raina.
